commands.review_summary:
The PR formalizes structured assistant error metadata and uses bounded provider details to classify unsupported-model responses as model-not-found across failover and sanitized user-facing error paths.

PR surface: Source +203, Tests +192. Total +395 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The exact reported OpenAI-compatible 400 response was exercised through the real transport in Crabbox, and the after-fix output showed the intended failover reason and sanitized user message.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected `maintainer` label requires explicit owner handling, but the reviewed patch itself has no narrow defect for an automated repair lane; the next action is ordinary maintainer review and merge of the linked fix.

target.title: fix(agents): classify structured unsupported model errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve structured provider error details for failover classification without changing stored/displayed `errorMessage`.
- Classify OpenAI-compatible `Not supported model <model-id>` details as `model_not_found` instead of schema/tool-payload failures.
- Add typed assistant error metadata and full-path regression coverage for thrown SDK-shaped errors, assistant `errorBody`, user-facing payloads, and OpenAI-compatible transport loopback.

Closes #92118

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible providers can return HTTP 400 bodies where `error.message` is `Param Incorrect` while `error.param` contains `Not supported model <model-id>`. OpenClaw now classifies that as `model_not_found` and avoids misleading schema/tool-payload copy.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_f55aeccc0a97` (`c7a.8xlarge`), run `run_de58983b891d`, using the dirty branch sync with a loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP server returning the exact reported payload.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Crabbox script started a local `/v1/chat/completions` loopback server returning `{"error":{"code":"400","message":"Param Incorrect","param":"Not supported model some-model-id"}}`, then called `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn()` and checked `resolveFailoverReasonFromError()`, `classifyAssistantFailoverReason()`, and `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText()`.

Evidence after fix: The Crabbox proof printed `OPENCLAW_92118_FIXED_PROOF` with `dependencyShapeClassification.reason: "model_not_found"`, `classifications.assistantFailoverReason: "model_not_found"`, and user-facing text `The selected model was not found by the provider. Check the model id or choose a different model.`

Observed result after fix: The exact reported payload no longer falls through to schema/tool-payload copy.

What was not tested: A live third-party Xiaomi/MiFY credentialed provider call was not run; the proof uses a local OpenAI-compatible loopback server with the exact reported response body and real OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible transport.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (ran twice after formatting; final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (2 shards, 616 tests passed)
- `../crabbox/bin/crabbox run --provider aws ...` (`run_de58983b891d`, `cbx_f55aeccc0a97`) for the reported loopback repro/fix proof
- `git diff --check`
